questions.questionText: Which hepatitis virus is a defective RNA virus that requires hepatitis B surface antigen for its assembly?
questions.explanation: Hepatitis D virus is a defective RNA virus that requires HBV infection and HBsAg for packaging and transmission. HAV and HEV are fecal-oral RNA viruses but not defective. HCV is an enveloped RNA virus that replicates independently.

questions.questionText: Owl’s eye intranuclear inclusions are characteristic of infection by:
questions.explanation: Cytomegalovirus classically causes enlarged cells with owl’s eye intranuclear inclusions. Herpes simplex causes multinucleated giant cells with Cowdry type A inclusions. Adenovirus can produce smudge cells. Rabies shows Negri bodies in neurons.

questions.questionText: Post-exposure prophylaxis after a category III dog bite in an unimmunized patient includes all EXCEPT:
questions.optionD: Tetanus toxoid as a substitute for rabies vaccine
questions.explanation: Tetanus prophylaxis may be indicated separately, but it cannot substitute for rabies vaccine in category III exposure. Correct rabies management includes immediate wound washing, rabies vaccine, and rabies immunoglobulin for previously unimmunized category III exposure. This is a high-yield practical question.
questions.source: Park, Immunization and Prophylaxis; Ananthanarayan, Rabies
